message:
v0.3.3: implied-rate sanity gate + breach log suppression

- Add unconditional implied-rate sanity gate in predictive.rs that
  blocks false alarms without requiring burst detector calibration.
  Two tiers: >1%/min at >40% free requires 0.90 confidence, >5%/min
  requires scaled confidence. Bypassed for imminent danger (<5min).
  Fixes false "disk full in 53m" on machines with 73%+ free space.

- Add recalibration_count to policy engine. After 3 breach→reset
  cycles, all breach/recalibration logging is suppressed since the
  guard is persistently miscalibrated for the workload. Counter
  resets when clean windows appear (workload changes).

- Fix failing_guard test helper to use observation_count=30
  (matching the production guard trigger threshold).

- Fix test free_pct values to be more realistic (80% free was being
  used in tests named "80% full").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.2"
+version = "0.3.3"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
